author: @John-s5h5w
text: 20 years ago I did some experiments using floats, weights and water to see if I could create a perpetual motion engine. Obviously it did not work, however I did manage to get gravity to create an imbalance in the machine and then gravity began to cancel out the imbalance, I then worked out that you could use gravity to cancel out the cancelling out effect of gravity, thus creating a stop/start machine. I did some very basic calculations to see if I could create enough leverage to rotate another machine that actually creates enough force to create a reasonable amount of energy using nothing but the force of gravity. I have scoured the internet for years to see if anyone has had the same or similar ideas as me. Everyone seems to struggle with the concept of using gravity to reset a machine powered by gravity. I have given this many years of study and I think I have come up with the closest thing to a  perpetual motion engine as possible. For years I have wanted to build a prototype, however, it has not been possible due to many things. If anyone is reading this could you contact me and I will go into more detail on how it works. I myself have no motivation anymore or resources.

author: @walterbaltzley4546
text:
The trick to making this technology work effectively at scale is to use negative pressure to lower the boiling point of a working fluid until it is close to room temperature.  Then, any amount of heat applied will cause the fluid to boil and can transfer that energy to a generator.  Unfortunately this means that your "boiler" must be hermetically sealed, making it mechanically impossible to attach a drive-shaft.  Enter MAGNETIC Coupling. 

Your generator and working fluid are housed in a hermetically sealed casing.  The turbine of the generator turns a large magnet inside the case and the pickup coil is on the outside.  This makes the system work more like a sterling engine - harnessing low-grade heat to boil a fluid and turn a magnet.  The fluid is then cooled to ambient air temperature, condenses, and the cycle continues.

author: @charleselertii6187
text:
Coil and magnet generators on VAWTs need a certain speed (cut-in rpm) to achieve a certain voltage to start charging a battery. If the VAWT   AlSO had a tribo generator attached to it, the VAWT could produce useful battery charge at low rpm below cut-in rpm.
So in effect, harvesting useful battery charging electricity with ANY rpm, or ANY movement. 
That is what I want to build. Charging a 12volt battery.... say, on a boat.
Chuck in Jensen Beach Florida.

author: @Forensic1Man
text: Hi Robert.  Have you considered using and experimenting with Piezo Film Sheets to generate electricity?  The material, piezoelectric polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) , is easy to use and it can generate a lot of electricity.  You just have to experiment with ways to continuously vibrate it.  It is very convenient to use.  Only one sheet does all the work.

author: @billschwandt1
text:
Mr. Murray-Smith, I thought windmills were kinda dumb but you kept talking about them so I turned my current project into the most complicated permanent magnet I have ever seen, but also a very efficient windmill.

I called it the monopole windmill and it's just a short but you will understand immediately.

I promise you have never seen a windmill like mine!

Kindest regards,

Bill

author: @gasonthebrain3738
text: I first saw that bird at Circular Quay in Sydney. It was 1963 and I was 6 years old at the time. I was amazed and puzzled. I never worked out how it worked, after thinking about it for many, many, many years. That bird had it’s own address in my brain. It is one of my most vivid, astonishing and enchanting memories. I finally found it’s secrets in a YouTube video not so long ago.
